message:
chore(beads): close ky4n (memtoull suffix support)

Closed via direct JSONL edit.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id": "frankenredis-kw56", "title": "redis parity: ACL channel patterns should be stored, rendered, and enforce revocation", "description": "Local AuthState::set_user only special-cases &* at crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs:835-839 and ignores literal and wildcard channel patterns. Upstream redis/tests/unit/acl.tcl exercises resetchannels with literals and patterns, expects ACL LIST to render resetchannels before channel specs, and expects subscribers to be dropped when channel permission is revoked. Implement channel-pattern storage, rendering, authorization, and revocation parity.", "status": "closed", "priority": 2, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-04-21T09:11:24.699383840Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-22T12:12:20.972210430Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-22T12:12:20.972210430Z", "close_reason": "Completed", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0}
 {"id": "frankenredis-kxn7", "title": "Add live Redis parity harness for stable MIGRATE cases", "description": "Add a focused core_migrate live differential test against vendored Redis for stable validation and NOKEY cases; refresh fixtures or parser behavior if the oracle exposes drift.", "status": "closed", "priority": 2, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-04-18T20:20:58.711281965Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-18T20:33:49.324435695Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-18T20:33:49.322613857Z", "close_reason": "Added live Redis MIGRATE parity coverage and fixed missing-key port validation to match Redis", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0}
+{"id": "frankenredis-ky4n", "title": "fr-runtime: CONFIG SET maxmemory rejects suffix forms (100mb, 1gb) that upstream accepts", "description": "fr-runtime's CONFIG SET maxmemory at crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs:7123 only accepts pure integers via parse_i64_arg. Upstream Redis 7.2 (config.c::numericParseString → util.c::memtoull) accepts suffix forms like '100mb', '1gb', '500k', '2gb', '512' (no suffix means bytes). The same suffix semantics apply to other memory-typed configs (repl-backlog-size, client-output-buffer-limit, proto-max-bulk-len, etc.).

Acceptance: 'CONFIG SET maxmemory 100mb' → OK on a non-cluster instance, matching vendored redis 7.2.4. Internally stores 100*1024*1024 = 104857600 bytes. Other suffix forms supported: '100kb', '100mb', '100gb' (1024-base); '100k', '100m', '100g' (1000-base); '100b' or '100' (bytes). Also CONFIG GET maxmemory should return the canonical decimal-byte form (matching upstream which always returns the integer byte count).

Reference: ad-hoc differential probe of fr (port 16700) vs vendored Redis 7.2.4 (port 16701).
  CONFIG SET maxmemory 100mb — fr 'ERR ...argument must be a memory value', redis 'OK'.

Fix: add a memtoull-equivalent helper at fr-runtime (or fr-config), use it for the maxmemory branch (CONFIG SET path at line 7123 + line 17504). Apply same helper to other memory-typed configs as follow-up.", "status": "closed", "priority": 2, "issue_type": "bug", "created_at": "2026-04-30T04:02:50.229710111Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-30T04:10:20.837213Z", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "assignee": "cc_1", "closed_at": "2026-04-30T04:10:20.837213Z", "close_reason": "CONFIG SET maxmemory now accepts memtoull suffix forms (100mb, 1gb, 500k, 100kb, 100b) matching upstream Redis 7.2 byte-for-byte. Other memory-typed configs (repl-backlog-size, etc.) still use parse_i64_arg — left as follow-up."}

 {"id": "frankenredis-nw8l", "title": "redis parity: ACL command/category grants should not lose implicit key access", "description": "The current fr-runtime full test run is red on acl_case_insensitive_command_matching, acl_allcommands_resets_granular_permissions, acl_category_based_permissions, acl_deny_category_overrides_allow_all, acl_deny_command_wins_over_allow_all, acl_per_command_allow_specific_commands, acl_per_command_deny_specific_commands, and acl_per_command_override_category_deny. The common failure mode is command/category ACL grants unexpectedly denying access to key 'k', which suggests recent ACL key-pattern/default handling regressed Redis's implicit allkeys behavior for these rule sets. Repair the runtime ACL permission evaluation so command/category grants preserve the expected key access semantics, and verify with the focused fr-runtime tests plus crate-level checks via rch.", "status": "closed", "priority": 2, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-04-21T19:10:30.452167624Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-21T19:13:44.389992065Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-21T19:13:44.387316901Z", "close_reason": "Completed", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0}
 {"id": "frankenredis-nwgw", "title": "Move SLOWLOG state to Store so dispatch_argv path returns real data", "description": "# Move SLOWLOG state to Store for Lua/dispatch_argv compatibility

## Problem
SLOWLOG GET/LEN/RESET in fr-command (the dispatch_argv path) returns empty arrays/0/OK because the slow log lives in Runtime's ServerState. Lua scripts calling redis.call(\"SLOWLOG\", \"GET\") get nothing.

## Design
Move the slowlog data structure from ServerState to Store:
- pub slowlog: VecDeque<SlowlogEntry>
- pub slowlog_id_counter: u64
- pub slowlog_log_slower_than_us: i64
- pub slowlog_max_len: usize

## Implementation
1. Define SlowlogEntry in fr-store (or move from fr-runtime):
   ```rust
   pub struct SlowlogEntry {
       pub id: u64,
       pub timestamp_sec: u64,
       pub duration_us: u64,
       pub argv: Vec<Vec<u8>>,
   }
   ```
2. Add the fields to Store
3. Add Store methods: record_slowlog(), get_slowlog(), slowlog_len(), reset_slowlog()
4. Update fr-command's slowlog_cmd to accept &mut Store and call these methods
5. Update fr-runtime to call store.record_slowlog() instead of server.record_slowlog()
6. Remove duplicate SlowlogEntry from runtime (or re-export from store)

## Why Store instead of Runtime
- Store is accessible from both dispatch_argv (Lua scripts, AOF replay) and the runtime
- The slow log is essentially per-database state, making Store the natural home
- This follows the same pattern we used for CONFIG GET/SET

## Migration path
- The runtime already has record_slowlog wired into execute_frame — just redirect to store
- slowlog_log_slower_than_us and slowlog_max_len are already CONFIG SET-able in runtime — redirect to store fields

## Testing
- Conformance: Lua script calls redis.call(\"SLOWLOG\", \"GET\") and gets real entries
- Unit test: record a slow command, verify SLOWLOG LEN returns 1

## Files to modify
- crates/fr-store/src/lib.rs (SlowlogEntry struct, Store fields, methods)
- crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs (slowlog_cmd accepts Store, reads real data)
- crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs (redirect slowlog recording to store)", "status": "closed", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-04-03T00:20:25.774798027Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-03T14:12:03.227702446Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-03T14:12:03.227428628Z", "close_reason": "Completed", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0}
 {"id":"frankenredis-nz2v","title":"fr-command: GEOPOS coordinate format diverges from upstream %.17Lg (16 vs 17 digits)","description":"GEOPOS emits coordinate doubles using Rust's f64 Display (Ryu shortest round-trip, typically 16 significant digits) where upstream Redis emits via addReplyHumanLongDouble formatted with %.17Lg (17 significant digits). Result: fr returns 0.9999999403953552 / 2.000000185646549 where Redis returns 0.99999994039535522 / 2.0000001856465488 for the same encoded geohash score. The actual lat/lng resolution is identical (same bit pattern decodes to the same point on the geoid), but the wire format differs in the trailing digit count.

Impact: low. Geo precision is dominated by the geohash bit budget (52 bits, ~0.6m at the equator), so the 17th digit is below noise. But this is a real wire-format divergence that breaks byte-equal diff tests.

Acceptance: GEOPOS / GEOSEARCH WITHCOORD format coordinate doubles with 17 significant digits in shortest-form (matches upstream %.17Lg). One reasonable Rust path: add a fr-command::format_redis_double(value: f64) -> String helper using a custom snprintf-style formatter, or use the printf crate. Conformance core_geo cases that currently XFAIL on GEOPOS lose the XFAIL.","status":"in_progress","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cc_1","created_at":"2026-04-29T22:08:31.295252959Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-30T02:38:46.110401303Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id": "frankenredis-nzaw", "title": "fr-command: CLUSTER SETSLOT and FAILOVER FORCE+TAKEOVER not handled", "description": "Two CLUSTER subcommand divergences from upstream Redis 7.2:

1. CLUSTER SETSLOT: any form returns 'ERR unknown subcommand SETSLOT'. Upstream accepts SETSLOT and (with cluster off) returns 'ERR This instance has cluster support disabled'. The four upstream forms are: 'SETSLOT <slot> STABLE', 'SETSLOT <slot> NODE <node-id>', 'SETSLOT <slot> MIGRATING <node-id>', 'SETSLOT <slot> IMPORTING <node-id>'.

2. CLUSTER FAILOVER FORCE TAKEOVER: fr-command::cluster_cmd::FAILOVER branch only accepts a single optional flag (FORCE or TAKEOVER, not both). Upstream accepts both modifiers together.

Acceptance: both cases return 'ERR This instance has cluster support disabled' on a non-cluster instance, matching vendored redis 7.2.4 byte-for-byte. Fix in fr-command::cluster_cmd: add a SETSLOT branch that validates argv shape then returns cluster_disabled_error when cluster is off; relax the FAILOVER branch arity check to allow FORCE+TAKEOVER together.", "status": "closed", "priority": 3, "issue_type": "bug", "created_at": "2026-04-30T03:53:32.811415876Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-30T04:01:28.801538Z", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "assignee": "cc_1", "closed_at": "2026-04-30T04:01:28.801538Z", "close_reason": "CLUSTER SETSLOT (4 forms) and CLUSTER FAILOVER FORCE+TAKEOVER now return 'ERR This instance has cluster support disabled' on a non-cluster instance, matching vendored redis 7.2.4 byte-for-byte."}
+{"id":"frankenredis-nzaw","title":"fr-command: CLUSTER SETSLOT and FAILOVER FORCE+TAKEOVER not handled","description":"Two CLUSTER subcommand divergences from upstream Redis 7.2:

1. CLUSTER SETSLOT: any form returns 'ERR unknown subcommand SETSLOT'. Upstream accepts SETSLOT and (with cluster off) returns 'ERR This instance has cluster support disabled'. The four upstream forms are: 'SETSLOT <slot> STABLE', 'SETSLOT <slot> NODE <node-id>', 'SETSLOT <slot> MIGRATING <node-id>', 'SETSLOT <slot> IMPORTING <node-id>'.

2. CLUSTER FAILOVER FORCE TAKEOVER: fr-command::cluster_cmd::FAILOVER branch only accepts a single optional flag (FORCE or TAKEOVER, not both). Upstream accepts both modifiers together.

Acceptance: both cases return 'ERR This instance has cluster support disabled' on a non-cluster instance, matching vendored redis 7.2.4 byte-for-byte. Fix in fr-command::cluster_cmd: add a SETSLOT branch that validates argv shape then returns cluster_disabled_error when cluster is off; relax the FAILOVER branch arity check to allow FORCE+TAKEOVER together.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-30T03:53:32.811415876Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-30T03:53:32.811415876Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id": "frankenredis-nzhr", "title": "redis parity: DEBUG needs NOSCRIPT guard and RESP error surface", "description": "Upstream metadata marks DEBUG as NOSCRIPT in legacy_redis_code/redis/src/commands/debug.json, but debug_cmd has no script_nesting_level guard at crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs:12794. Separately, the unknown-subcommand parity tests at crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs:25774 and :25788 expect a RESP Error frame, while debug_cmd currently returns CommandError::Custom at crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs:12913. Bring DEBUG into line with Redis script restrictions and reply semantics.", "status": "closed", "priority": 2, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-04-21T02:39:59.248821257Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-21T03:13:03.803235604Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-21T03:13:03.800889203Z", "close_reason": "Completed", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0}
 {"id": "frankenredis-o0sn", "title": "[GOLDEN] Sentinel list replies should be deterministic", "description": "testing-golden-artifacts found SENTINEL MASTERS/REPLICAS/SENTINELS build arrays from HashMap::values(), making response order nondeterministic and hostile to golden/conformance fixtures. Sort list responses by instance name and add a deterministic golden.", "status": "closed", "priority": 2, "issue_type": "bug", "assignee": "cod_1", "created_at": "2026-04-28T20:50:41.132596051Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-29T00:42:18.093810442Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-29T00:42:18.091884630Z", "close_reason": "Completed: SENTINEL list replies now sort by instance name and golden coverage freezes deterministic masters output.", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["fr-sentinel", "golden"]}
